diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coffee-ground vomitus is altered blood and localises the bleeding source to the upper GI tract.
Haematemesis does not occur with an isolated lower-GI source.
Melaena reflects digested blood from an upper-GI source of sufficient volume and transit time.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Black stools from oral iron or bismuth (pseudomelaena)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Iron/bismuth can darken stool, but the accompanying haematemesis and shock make true melaena the correct interpretation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemic shock from acute blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural collapse indicates the circulating volume has fallen enough that compensatory mechanisms are failing — early haemorrhagic shock.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemic shock from acute blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor and sweating reflect sympathetic compensation and reduced perfusion; carer-reportable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.
Antecedent epigastric burning pain is consistent with peptic ulceration as the bleeding lesion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemic shock from acute blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hypotension with compensatory tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from reported near-syncope, pallor and weakness, not a measured BP.
Chronic heavy alcohol use predisposes to portal hypertension and varices — an important-not-to-miss upper-GI bleeding source.
Alcohol is also a mucosal irritant contributing to gastritis/ulceration.
NSAIDs cause gastric mucosal injury and are a leading precipitant of peptic-ulcer haemorrhage.
Absence of blood thinners does not lower urgency, but is relevant to reversal decisions in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (4 seconds), weak thready pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemic shock from acute blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged capillary refill and a weak pulse indicate poor peripheral perfusion — sealed clinician examination finding, not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination confirming melaena on the glove

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: DRE-confirmed melaena objectively corroborates GI blood loss — sealed clinician examination, impossible via chat.

investigation_details.expected_result: Falling haemoglobin consistent with acute blood loss (may lag early)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of blood loss and transfusion need cannot be quantified

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute upper gastrointestinal bleed with hypovolaemic shock
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A falling haemoglobin quantifies the haemorrhage — a hospital blood test, never available in real time in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Actively bleeding mucosal lesion / visible vessel on upper endoscopy

investigation_details.expected_result: Actively bleeding mucosal lesion or non-bleeding visible vessel identified and treated endoscopically
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Bleeding source cannot be identified or definitively controlled

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct endoscopic visualisation is the gold-standard diagnostic and therapeutic step — a hospital procedure, never a telehealth capability.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Major upper-GI bleed with haemodynamic compromise
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage with hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Haematemesis plus melaena plus postural near-syncope plus pallor/diaphoresis together constitute an unmistakable emergency requiring immediate ambulance escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency-ambulance transfer for hospital resuscitation and urgent upper GI endoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive investigation and treatment (endoscopy after resuscitation) can only occur in hospital; the telehealth expectation is to arrange this immediately via 000, not to order tests remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea/electrolytes, coagulation profile, group and crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify blood loss, assess renal effect of GI blood, screen coagulopathy and prepare for transfusion — performed in hospital on arrival, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home blood-pressure or pulse measurement requested from the patient at first contact
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do NOT delay escalation to obtain a home reading; the clinical picture already mandates a 000 call, and time spent measuring vitals is time not calling an ambulance.
